(born **Kam Tong Chun**; May 27, 1918 – October 18, 2002) was an American [police officer](/wiki/Police_officer "Police officer") and actor, best known for his role as Chin Ho Kelly, a police [detective](/wiki/Detective "Detective") on the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") [television network](/wiki/Television_network "Television network") [series](/wiki/Television_show "Television show") *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.
when he was hired for *Hawaii Five-O*.

**Kam Fong Chun** (born **Kam Tong Chun**; May 27, 1918 – October 18, 2002) was an American [police officer](/wiki/Police_officer "Police officer") and actor, best known for his role as Chin Ho Kelly, a police [detective](/wiki/Detective "Detective") on the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") [television network](/wiki/Television_network "Television network") [series](/wiki/Television_show "Television show") *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kam_Fong_Chun&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life")]

Kam Fong Chun was born in the [Kalihi](/wiki/Kalihi "Kalihi") neighborhood of [Honolulu](/wiki/Honolulu "Honolulu"), [Hawaii](/wiki/Territory_of_Hawaii "Territory of Hawaii"). A 1938 graduate of [President William McKinley High School](/wiki/President_William_McKinley_High_School "President William McKinley High School"),[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) he worked at [Pearl Harbor](/wiki/Pearl_Harbor "Pearl Harbor") shipyard in his 20s as a boiler maker and was a witness to the [attack by the Japanese on December 7, 1941](/wiki/Attack_on_Pearl_Harbor "Attack on Pearl Harbor"). After the death of his first wife and two eldest children in 1944, he applied for a job as a [police officer](/wiki/Police_officer "Police officer") at the [Honolulu Police Department](/wiki/Honolulu_Police_Department "Honolulu Police Department"). He served there for 16 years. After his retirement from the police force, he worked as a disc jockey and sold real estate in addition to doing community theater.

Chun's life was filled with tragedies. His father had an affair, which led to his parents' divorce and the splitting of the family. The two eldest children went with their father and the younger five, including seven-year-old Chun, lived with their mother. The affair also led to Chun's father being forced out of the family business by his paternal grandfather, which left the family in poverty.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Chun watched a brother burn to death as he was painting the family home and someone lit a match.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) On June 8, 1944, Chun lost his family in a freak air disaster that devastated their home in Honolulu. Two B-25 bombers collided over the Chun residence, killing wife Esther, four-year-old daughter Marilyn and two-year-old son Donald.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

Chun later married Gladys Lindo in 1949. They had two sons, [Dennis](/wiki/Dennis_Chun "Dennis Chun") and Dickson, and daughters, Brenda and Valerie.

### Stage name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kam_Fong_Chun&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Stage name")]

Chun's stage name came from a misunderstanding of his first name by his first teacher, who taught him to write Kam Fong Chun instead of his birth name, Kam Tong Chun. Due to confusion as he got older, he later legalized his name to the former. [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") asked him to shorten his name to **Kam Fong** when he was hired for *Hawaii Five-O*.

### Proposed 1997 *Five-O* revival

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kam_Fong_Chun&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Proposed 1997 Five-O revival")]

Talk had centered around a remake or a feature film version of the show for years. In 1997, CBS and [Stephen J. Cannell](/wiki/Stephen_J._Cannell "Stephen J. Cannell") (*[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")*, *[Baretta](/wiki/Baretta "Baretta")*, *[The Commish](/wiki/The_Commish "The Commish")*) collaborated on a pilot for a possible new *Five-O* series. The pilot would introduce some of the new cast and feature former regulars from the original series, including Fong. According to *Five-O* fan and author of a book on the show, Karen Rhodes, Fong was asked to reprise his role and appear in the pilot. Neither Fong nor any of the other regulars told Cannell that Chin Ho had been killed off at the end of the tenth season. This was only discovered after all of Fong's scenes had been shot, and to excise him from the project would have caused delays and overruns in cost. Hoping that CBS executives would not remember the one episode out of hundreds, Cannell screened the pilot.

His son [Dennis Chun](/wiki/Dennis_Chun "Dennis Chun") had a recurring role in the [2010 reboot](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-0_(2010_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-0 (2010 TV series)") as HPD Sgt. Duke Lukela.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Beginning with the [8th season](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-0_(2010_TV_series,_season_8) "Hawaii Five-0 (2010 TV series, season 8)") he was promoted to a series regular.

### Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kam_Fong_Chun&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Death")]

Kam Fong Chun died from [lung cancer](/wiki/Lung_cancer "Lung cancer") on October 18, 2002, at the age of 84.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kam_Fong_Chun&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Filmography")]

* *Ghost of the China Sea* (1958) — Pvt. Hakashima
* *[The Lost Missile](/wiki/The_Lost_Missile "The Lost Missile")* (1958) — Chinese Officer (uncredited)
* *[Cry for Happy](/wiki/Cry_for_Happy "Cry for Happy")* (1961) — Chin, Sailor (uncredited)
* *[Gidget Goes Hawaiian](/wiki/Gidget_Goes_Hawaiian "Gidget Goes Hawaiian")* (1961) — Hotel Night Clerk (uncredited)
* *[Seven Women from Hell](/wiki/Seven_Women_from_Hell "Seven Women from Hell")* (1961) — Burly Guard (uncredited)
* *[Diamond Head](/wiki/Diamond_Head_(film) "Diamond Head (film)")* (1962) — Loe Kim Lee (uncredited)
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (1968–1978) — Det. Chin Ho Kelly
* *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")* (1982–1985) — Kanki / Kam Chung
* *Goodbye Paradise* (1991) — Old man Yung
* *Hawaii Five-O* (1997) — Det. Chin Ho Kelly (final film role)
